hallucinations.description: The paper claims to perform neural theorem proving experiments using Lean 4 verifier feedback, error taxonomies from formal proofs, and curriculum learning on proof errors. However, the provided code implements a completely unrelated toy classification task: random 64-dim feature vectors are generated from text hashing or procedural noise, with 8 synthetic error categories injected as feature biases. The model is a simple MLP classifier on these features, not a theorem prover or proof generator. No Lean 4, no actual proofs, no verifier feedback, and no mathematical reasoning occurs.
hallucinations.evidence: Code in ProofErrorDataset, generate_synthetic_proof_data, process_huggingface_dataset, ImprovedETCLModel (MLP encoder + classifier), train_model_improved, and ablation scripts. All datasets (synthetic/gsm8k/sciq/winogrande) are turned into 8-class error category classification with injected features; no theorem proving logic.

hallucinations.description: Reported metrics (e.g., GSM8K 84.8% vs 84.3%, SciQ 85.5% vs 83.3%, generalization gaps, ablation tables) do not correspond to the actual experiments. The code produces different loss/accuracy curves and the task is classification accuracy on 8 synthetic classes, not theorem proving success rates or proof repair.
hallucinations.evidence: Figures 1-3, Table 1, and main results section claim specific validation accuracies and gaps on GSM8K/SciQ/WinoGrande. Code shows validation accuracies ~0.84-0.87 on the classification task with different dynamics (e.g., label smoothing ablations, curriculum effects) and no matching numbers or task.

hallucinations.description: Multiple citations are placeholders (GPT-f (?), DeepSeek-Prover (?), Goedel-Prover (?), miniF2F (?), PutnamBench (?), Lean 4 (?), etc.) or fabricated references that do not correspond to real papers.
hallucinations.evidence: Introduction, Related Work, and Experiments sections contain repeated **?** placeholders and non-standard citations (e.g., Goedel-Prover-V2, Leanabell-Prover-V2, APOLLO) that do not resolve to verifiable academic works.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations were found. The paper fabricates a theorem-proving narrative and results around a toy 8-class classification experiment on synthetic features. Methodology, results, and citations are all inconsistent with the actual code and experiments.
